B002.summary: Python does not support the unary prefix increment operator (`++`)

B003.summary: Assigning to `os.environ` doesn't clear the environment

B004.summary: Using `hasattr(x, "__call__")` to test if x is callable is unreliable. Use `callable(x)` for consistent results.

B005.summary: Using `.strip()` with multi-character strings is misleading

B006.summary: Do not use mutable data structures for argument defaults

B007.summary: Loop control variable `{name}` not used within loop body

B008.summary: Do not perform function call `{name}` in argument defaults; instead, perform the call within the function, or read the default from a module-level singleton variable

B009.summary: Do not call `getattr` with a constant attribute value. It is not any safer than normal property access.

B010.summary: Do not call `setattr` with a constant attribute value. It is not any safer than normal property access.

B011.summary: Do not `assert False` (`python -O` removes these calls), raise `AssertionError()`

B012.summary: `{name}` inside `finally` blocks cause exceptions to be silenced

B013.summary: A length-one tuple literal is redundant in exception handlers

B015.summary: Pointless comparison. Did you mean to assign a value? Otherwise, prepend `assert` or remove it.

B016.summary: Cannot raise a literal. Did you intend to return it or raise an Exception?

B018.summary: Found useless expression. Either assign it to a variable or remove it.

B019.summary: Use of `functools.lru_cache` or `functools.cache` on methods can lead to memory leaks

B020.summary: Loop control variable `{name}` overrides iterable it iterates

B021.summary: f-string used as docstring. Python will interpret this as a joined string, rather than a docstring.

B022.summary: No arguments passed to `contextlib.suppress`. No exceptions will be suppressed and therefore this context manager is redundant

B023.summary: Function definition does not bind loop variable `{name}`

B024.summary: `{name}` is an abstract base class, but it has no abstract methods or properties

B026.summary: Star-arg unpacking after a keyword argument is strongly discouraged

B027.summary: `{name}` is an empty method in an abstract base class, but has no abstract decorator

B029.summary: Using `except* ():` with an empty tuple does not catch anything; add exceptions to handle

B030.summary: `except*` handlers should only be exception classes or tuples of exception classes

B031.summary: Using the generator returned from `itertools.groupby()` more than once will do nothing on the second usage

B032.summary: Possible unintentional type annotation (using `:`). Did you mean to assign (using `=`)?

B033.summary: Sets should not contain duplicate item `{value}`

B034.summary: `{method}` should pass `{param_name}` and `flags` as keyword arguments to avoid confusion due to unintuitive argument positions

B039.summary: Do not use mutable data structures for `ContextVar` defaults

B901.summary: Using `yield` and `return {value}` in a generator function can lead to confusing behavior

B903.summary: Class could be dataclass or namedtuple

B904.summary: Within an `except*` clause, raise exceptions with `raise ... from err` or `raise ... from None` to distinguish them from errors in exception handling

B909.summary: Mutation to loop iterable `{name}` during iteration
